channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: New Clive Barker's Hellraiser: Revival Dev Diary Shows Off Terrifying Cenobites
item.content:encoded:
Developer and publisher Saber Interactive has released a brand new dev diary for upcoming horror game Clive Barker's Hellraiser: Revival, in which the studio shows off its rendering of the undeniably creepy Cenobites in the game.

First announced back in July last year, Hellraiser: Revival is the first single-player horror game based on the iconic series, and if there's one thing Saber had to get right, it was the Cenobites and their leader Pinhead.

The new dev diary certainly starts off on the right foot; it begins with an introduction to Pinhead and the Cenobites by none other than Doug Bradley, who's played the legendary character across several Hellraiser movies, as well as appearances in other media like asymmetrical multiplayer game Dead by Daylight.

In the dev diary, Saber says that it tried to return to a "more monastic" interpretation of Pinhead, one that interprets the character as someone who's "searching for enlightenment through sensation", which sounds pretty faithful to the original Hellraiser media.

Of course, Pinhead isn't the only Cenobite who's appearing in Hellraiser: Revival, and so the dev diary talks about Chatterer, Butterball, and the Female Cenobite as well. If you're a Hellraiser fan, it looks like you're going to get a lot out of this one.

You can check out the new dev diary for Clive Barker's Hellraiser: Revival, which features several Saber staffers (and Bradley, of course) going into detail about Cenobites and the way they've been brought to life in the game, right here.

Seeing as you can no longer obtain Dead by Daylight's interpretation of the Cenobites (although you can still play as them if you already own them), Revival is your best chance to experience the world of the movies and the books in video game form.

You'll be able to check out Clive Barker's Hellraiser: Revival when it launches across PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S later this year.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/clive-barkers-hellraiser-revival-cenobites
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker PC Requirements Revealed: 5090 for 4K
item.content:encoded:
Publisher Bandai Namco and developer Rebel Wolves have announced the PC system requirements for upcoming action RPG The Blood of Dawnwalker, and this one's going to demand some serious heft from your PC to run at 4K.

First unveiled all the way back in January last year, The Blood of Dawnwalker is slated to release in September, which should give you plenty of time to upgrade your machine ahead of its launch (if you've got the cash, of course).

As you'd probably expect, The Blood of Dawnwalker isn't going to tax your specs too much if you're not fussy about resolution or frame rate, but if you're looking to play at the highest resolution and with decent FPS, then you'd better have the goods (or, perhaps, your PC had better have the goods).

Here's the full list of PC specs for The Blood of Dawnwalker. Be aware all of these presets will require 16GB of RAM minimum, as well as 60GB of SSD space and Windows 10 at the lowest.

Minimum (1080p, 30fps, Low)

  • CPU: Intel Core i7-8700K or AMD Ryzen 7 3700X
  • GPU: Nvidia GeForce RTX 3050 or GTX 1070, or AMD Radeon RX Vega-56, or Intel Arc A580
  • VRAM: 6GB

Recommended (1080p, 60fps, High)

  • CPU: Intel Core i5-13600 or AMD Ryzen 9 7900X
  • GPU: Nvidia GeForce RTX 5060 or AMD Radeon RX 6800-XT
  • VRAM: 12GB

Recommended (1440p, 60fps, High)

  • CPU: Intel Core i5-13600 or AMD Ryzen 9 7900X
  • GPU: Nvidia GeForce RTX 4070 Ti or AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT
  • VRAM: 12GB

Ultra (1440p, 60fps, Ultra)

  • CPU: Intel Core i5-13600K or AMD Ryzen 9 7950X
  • GPU: Nvidia GeForce RTX 4080 or AMD Radeon RX 7900-XTX
  • VRAM: 16GB

Ultra (4K, 60fps, Ultra)

  • CPU: Intel Core i5-13600K or AMD Ryzen 9 7950X
  • GPU: Nvidia GeForce RTX 5090 (sorry, AMD users)
  • VRAM: 16GB

The Blood of Dawnwalker launches on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S on September 3rd. You can check out our preview of the game here.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
